evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints (oauth2/authorize, oauth2/token, /api/v10/applications/{id}/guilds/{gid}/commands) are internally consistent with Discord's known API surface. Scopes identify/guilds correct. No version claim with date though API v10 referenced. Docs unavailable — internally consistent + version mentioned ≈ 3-4.
evidence.executability: Uses httpx.utils.quote which does not exist in httpx (httpx has no utils.quote helper; should use urllib.parse.quote). This will raise AttributeError at import-time when building OAUTH_AUTHORIZE_URL. Also @app.on_event('startup') is deprecated in newer FastAPI but still works. The httpx.utils.quote bug is a hard runtime failure → executability capped low.
evidence.completeness: All 6 pieces present: (a) Developer Portal acquisition steps, (b) bash install commands, (c) explicit .env template with exact names matching code, (d) backend code, (e) frontend code, (f) test command (pytest example + uvicorn run). Minor: test snippet imports backend.main but file path is backend/main.py without __init__.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites docs.discord.com/developers/... which is not the canonical Discord docs domain (canonical is discord.com/developers/docs). FastAPI, CRA, Vite official docs cited inline near relevant code. Mix of official + third-party (stackoverflow, curity, obsidiansecurity). Discord docs URL appears fabricated/incorrect domain.

rubric_notes: httpx.utils.quote is a real bug — that module/attribute doesn't exist. Discord docs domain 'docs.discord.com/developers' is non-canonical (real is discord.com/developers/docs).
